title: resinous dense portuguese blackberries bowl
description: a sweet, comforting dessert featuring the rich flavors of portugal in every bite. made with fresh blackberries, sweet candies, and creamy milk, this dish is a delight for the senses.

ingredients.name: fresh blackberries
ingredients.notes: fresh from the market, or frozen if out of season

ingredients.name: golden caramel candies
ingredients.notes: chopped into small pieces for easier mixing

ingredients.name: honey
ingredients.notes: straight from the hive for a hint of floral sweetness

ingredients.name: all-purpose flour
ingredients.notes: dried, unbleached, and unenriched for a delicate crumb

ingredients.name: baking powder
ingredients.notes: double-acting for a light and fluffy texture

ingredients.name: salt
ingredients.notes: pure sea salt for a touch of savory depth

ingredients.name: unsalted butter
ingredients.notes: softened to room temperature for easy mixing

ingredients.name: shredded coconut
ingredients.notes: unsweetened and unsulphured for a hint of tropical flavor

instructions: in a blender or food processor, combine the blackberries, honey, and 1 tablespoon of the softened butter. blend until smooth and well combined.
in a separate bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, and salt. add in the remaining 1 tablespoon of softened butter and mix until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
fold the dry ingredients into the wet ingredients until just combined, being careful not to overmix.
transfer the batter to a 6-inch mason jar or ramekin and smooth the top.
place the jar or ramekin in a large baking dish and add enough hot water to come halfway up the sides.
bake for 25 minutes, or until the edges are golden brown and the center is set.
remove from the oven and let cool for 5 minutes before serving.
invert the jar or ramekin onto a plate and serve warm, topped with chopped caramel candies and shredded coconut, if desired.
to make the caramel sauce, combine the golden caramel candies and 1 tablespoon of whole milk in a small saucepan over low heat. stir until the candies have melted and the mixture is smooth.
remove from the heat and let cool slightly before drizzling over the dessert.

to make the whipped cream, combine the whole milk and 1 tablespoon of unsalted butter in a blender or food processor. blend until smooth and well combined. add in 1 tablespoon of honey and blend until stiff peaks form.
transfer the whipped cream to a piping bag or a zip-top plastic bag and pipe onto the dessert, if desired.
to finish, sprinkle the top with shredded coconut and drizzle with caramel sauce, if desired.
note: the baking time may vary depending on the size and shape of your mason jar or ramekin. be sure to check the dessert frequently to avoid overcooking.
